rationale.R1.1: The open-ended request invites assessment of the thesis, contribution framing, and whether the claimed framework is conceptually coherent.
rationale.R1.2: Evaluating what one thinks of the passage centrally includes checking whether the mechanism, ablations, and conclusions follow logically.
rationale.R1.3: The artifact makes empirical claims about BLEU, COMET, and ablations, so assessing whether those claims support the stated conclusions is central.
rationale.R1.6: An open-ended reaction to a draft passage naturally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, and register.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection should preserve uncertainty because the artifact is only a short draft excerpt and not the full paper or evidence base.
rationale.RX.2: The delegation requires attention to whether the artifact's claims are stronger than the reported evidence warrants.
rationale.RX.3: The projection must stay within assessing requested responsibilities and not actually critique, rewrite, or verify the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: The projection should account for risks from unsupported claims about translation performance and generalization.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should remain traceable to the delegation wording and the specific claims present in the artifact.
